user_message: Help me build pipeline hygiene for Humongous Insurance. This is a renewal account with declining consumption.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-25 14:13 EST
Subject: Re: pipeline hygiene Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about pipeline hygiene. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
Date: 2024-02-19 17:13 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Humongous Insurance pipeline hygiene

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about pipeline hygiene. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-10 10:05 EST
Subject: Re: Re: pipeline hygiene Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about pipeline hygiene. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: pipeline hygiene Discussion - Humongous Insurance
Date: 2024-03-15
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:01:30] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into pipeline hygiene.

[00:07:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:10:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:13:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:15:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): Let me add some context -- we've been working on security compliance and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:17:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on vendor dependencies and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:20:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Humongous Insurance: there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Stakeholder Map Generation template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct pipeline hygiene decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
